$comment: Contract for the structured arrays + triage_summary in Diagnostic_State.meta.json. items.$schema_ref names the per-element schema each array must satisfy (a stdlib extension to JSON Schema, read by structured_findings.py). The validator additionally enforces the cross-field rule — a non-empty findings[] requires a triage_summary whose counts equal the findings[] severity tally — which plain schema cannot express. Other sidecar fields are permitted (additionalProperties true).
title: APODICTIC diagnostic-state sidecar (apodictic.diagnostic-state.v1)

complexity_signals.$comment: Adaptive Mid-Run Mode Escalation (docs/adaptive-mode-escalation.md): Tier-1 complexity signals Pass 0/1 record so `validate.sh escalation-check` can decide whether to recommend escalating the execution mode before Tier 2. tier1_finding_count is NOT stored — escalation-check computes it from the ledger's F-P0-/F-P1- blocks.

synthesis_coverage.$comment: Synthesis Coverage Disclosure, M1 (docs/synthesis-regrounding.md; complexity_signals additive pattern — no new schema file, no version bump): the sidecar projection of the [Project]_Synthesis_Read_Manifest_[runlabel].md written BEFORE the editorial letter (run-synthesis.md §Processing Protocol step 9b). `coverage` must equal the letter's `<!-- coverage: ok|degraded -->` title-block marker; the artifacts_* tallies and spans_outside_active_context must equal the manifest rows; `provenance` is dispatch-derived (sequential/hybrid/swarm — the orchestrator wrote the manifest at dispatch) or declared (single-agent only — no read-event log exists, and the letter says so in pinned language). verification_excerpt_count is multi-agent only (null in single-agent); estimated_context_utilization is single-agent only (null in multi-agent, percent of the detected window). Reconciled by `validate.sh synthesis-coverage <run_folder>` — an unfilled template stub (empty provenance+coverage) reads as absent (V1, advisory-first), a present-but-malformed value FAILS reconciliation (V3, blocking). Filled on every run that writes a full editorial letter; leave the stub untouched otherwise.

dispatch_log.$comment: Model-Capacity Exploitation M1 — dispatch observability (docs/model-capacity-dispatch-log.md; complexity_signals / synthesis_coverage additive pattern — no new schema file, no version bump). One entry per delegated-agent dispatch the parent orchestrator issued during the run (Execution Protocol steps 7 and 10, run-core.md): it records WHICH model each dispatched step was issued to — the single join key between model identity and every outcome signal already on disk. PROVENANCE HONESTY, binding on all consumers (prose, `dispatch-record --report`, any future M2/M3): `provenance: dispatch-derived` attests the dispatch INSTRUCTION — the model parameter the parent requested — it is PARENT-REQUESTED, NOT PLATFORM-VERIFIED; no host API attests which model actually served the subagent. This is deliberately WEAKER than synthesis_coverage's dispatch-derived manifest (whose rows are checkable file paths reconciled by V2-V4); dispatch_log has no equivalent reconciliation substrate, and neither the schema nor `dispatch-record` claims one. `provenance: declared` = a step that ran inline with no dispatch event (no-shell hosts, run-core.md:392; or a genuinely inline step) — legitimate under any execution_mode, since provenance tracks the host's per-entry dispatch capability, NOT the run's mode. `model_tag` is a tag from the output-structure.md model-tag table, or the literal `unknown` (the documented derivation fallback, output-structure.md:183 — a sanctioned value, never a lie). The log records the model TAG, never a 'tier' label (tier<->model mapping is a judgment that belongs in future M3, not the durable record). Reconciled by `validate.sh dispatch-record <run_folder>` (R1 presence advisory-first; R2 pass-artifact + synthesis-letter coverage per the step satisfaction map; R3 tag vocabulary; R4 shape; R5 escalation cross-check) — advisory-first (WARN), --strict escalates; key-absent = pre-adoption grandfather (silent pass), present-but-[] = a post-adoption recording failure (R1 fires). Additive: leave absent on runs that do not record dispatches.

step.$comment: v1 grammar: pass<N> | pass0+1 (combined triage subagent, run-core.md:292) | synthesis | all-passes (single-agent's one subagent). Deferred extension (accepted-if-recorded, NOT reconciled by R2): audit:<audit-id>, prerequisite:<audit-id>, refutation. Any id outside both sets = unknown step kind (R4 WARN).

model_tag.$comment: a tag from the output-structure.md model-tag table, or the literal `unknown`.

execution_mode.$comment: single-agent | sequential | hybrid | swarm — the mode AT dispatch (may change once, at the Tier-1/2 escalation seam).

max_turns.$comment: host-dependent turn budget; null when the host exposes none.

provenance.$comment: dispatch-derived (parent-requested, not platform-verified) | declared (inline, no dispatch event).

execution.$comment: Runner-Governed Execution state. Increment 5 (state_version 2): gate_events[] is the canonical append-only gate-decision log; phase/allowed_next/pending_gate/finding_states are a recomputable resume pointer (== fold(gate_events), asserted by run_gate.py --check-state). gates (per-phase result map) is DEPRECATED — read only when state_version < 2 (legacy fallback).

state_version.$comment: 2 = gate_events model; absent/1 = legacy gates-map sidecar. Readers branch on this.

gate_events.$comment: append-only gate-decision log. Nested under execution, so validate_sidecar_obj's top-level walker does not reach it; gate-state (run_gate.py --check-state) iterates and validates per item.

pending_gate.$comment: the gate /start must resolve next (earliest open exception); OMITTED when none (never null).

gates.$comment: DEPRECATED by gate_events; read only when state_version < 2.

finding_states.$comment: finding_id -> lifecycle state (locked | delivered | revised); advanced forward-only by the gate.

attested.$comment: DEPRECATED by gate_events[].attested_items; legacy phase -> confirmed-items map.
